turns.user_message: I tried doing a quick meditation session to reset my mood, but it just made me sit with my own thoughts longer. This kind of heavy feeling used to not bother me at all, but now it just sits right on my chest. I keep telling myself to just push through it like I always do, but the weight feels completely different now. I honestly don't know why I even bother trying to fix it when nothing seems to shift.

I tried forcing myself to watch a funny movie to distract my brain, but I just ended up staring at the screen feeling completely empty. It is so frustrating because this lingering sense of inadequacy used to not bother me, but lately it follows me everywhere I go. I keep catching myself comparing my quiet moments to other people's loud successes. I just feel like I am constantly falling short, and the gap keeps getting wider every single day.

I tried writing down a list of my recent wins to boost my confidence, but my pen just hovered over the paper until I gave up. This crushing doubt used to not bother me in the slightest, but now it completely paralyzes my every thought. I keep replaying every minor misstep until I feel entirely worthless and completely drained. I am starting to accept that this heavy fog is just my permanent reality, and I do not know how to carry it anymore.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This used to not bother the speaker.
